title: Pre-Labor Awareness
subtitle: Staying prepared and attentive
description: As you approach the final stage, your body may show signs of preparing for labor. Increased awareness of physical changes, minimizing strain, and staying prepared can help you remain calm and comfortable.
what_to_expect: Heightened awareness of body changes and sensations
Increased pressure and discomfort in the lower body
Greater focus on readiness for labor
daily_safety_tips: Stay attentive to new or unusual physical changes
Avoid unnecessary physical effort and prioritize rest
Keep essential items and contacts easily accessible
movement_guidelines: Limit movement to gentle and necessary activities
Avoid any movement that causes discomfort or strain
Use support or assistance when moving
environmental_awareness: Ensure surroundings are safe, stable, and easy to navigate
Keep pathways clear and accessible at all times
Continue avoiding exposure to harmful chemicals or smoke
travel_safety: Stay close to accessible care or support if possible
Use maximum support and comfort while seated
important_note: Staying aware of your body and minimizing strain can help you feel more prepared.
reassurance: With careful attention and support, you can feel more confident during this stage.
medical_disclaimer: Consult a qualified healthcare professional before starting or modifying any physical activity.

diabetes.personalized_description: Maintaining consistent meals and minimal activity supports stable blood sugar levels during this stage.
movement_guidelines: Engage in minimal gentle movement when comfortable
Maintain consistent meal timing and hydration
Monitor your body's response to reduced activity
diabetes.safety_focus: Watch for fatigue, dizziness, or changes in energy levels.
diabetes.reassurance: Balanced routines support stability and comfort.

anemia.personalized_description: High fatigue levels require maximum rest and minimal exertion.
movement_guidelines: Limit activity strictly to essential tasks
Take frequent and extended rest periods
anemia.safety_focus: Be aware of dizziness, weakness, or breathlessness.
anemia.reassurance: Rest and minimal effort help maintain comfort.

hypertension.personalized_description: Maintaining a calm and low-stress routine supports stable blood pressure.
movement_guidelines: Avoid stressful or physically demanding situations
Engage in minimal and controlled movement
hypertension.safety_focus: Be mindful of stress levels and physical strain.
hypertension.reassurance: A calm and steady approach supports well-being.

thyroid.personalized_description: Consistent routines and minimal activity support metabolic balance during this phase.
movement_guidelines: Maintain a steady and minimal activity pattern
thyroid.safety_focus: Be aware of unusual fatigue or energy fluctuations.

multi_condition_blend.combined_message: When managing multiple conditions, prioritizing rest, awareness, and minimal movement becomes critical during this stage.
priority_guidelines: Avoid unnecessary physical effort or strain
Maintain minimal and essential movement only
Take frequent rest breaks and stay hydrated
multi_condition_blend.reassurance: Careful rest and awareness help maintain comfort and safety.
